label: Generative modeling

node.label: Generative modeling
node.definition: A model learns a data distribution or transformation process capable of producing new samples.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Generative modeling is modeled as depending on Probability theory, Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs and feeding downstream nodes such as Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy, Autoregressive / next-step prediction, Reconstruction objectives.
examples: atlas downstream link to Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy
atlas downstream link to Autoregressive / next-step prediction
atlas downstream link to Reconstruction objectives
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Generative modeling has a `uses` dependency on Probability theory because Probability theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Generative modeling has a `uses` dependency on Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs because Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Generative modeling has a `uses` dependency on Latent variables & hidden state because Latent variables & hidden state is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Generative modeling has a `uses` dependency on Optimization theory because Optimization theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
